description:
My brother and I first saw this bird swimming fairly close to the inlet shore, from about 150 ft away. Since the reservoir is very low at this time of the year, it is a decent walk to get to the waters edge. As I was walking along the mudflats, I immediately noticed a large loon in breeding plumage with a massive yellow bill. 


Description of the bird: Loon with a large creamy yellow bill angling up slightly at the end. Overall black body, with striking white checkering patterns on the back. White ring on neck, dark red eye, and a steep forehead. White breast and below. Much larger than a Common loon, differentiated by bill color and size. Called a couple times at a distance. 


Diving frequently about 300 ft from shore, only staying up for 20-30 seconds between dives. Strong winds made observations a bit difficult.
